name: Generate blog drafts with MySQL and WordPress in real time

options.systemMessage: You are the coordinator for a cross-posting pipeline. Break the request into sub-tasks, delegate each to the matching specialist tool, then merge results into one coherent answer. Never call the same specialist twice for one request.

parameters.description: Delegate research sub-tasks for a post to the Research specialist workflow.
Delegate draft sub-tasks for a post to the Draft specialist workflow.
Delegate review sub-tasks for a post to the Review specialist workflow.

parameters.text: =✅ Generate blog drafts with MySQL and WordPress in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Stamp & Shape Post

### Stamp & Shape Post

Shapes the item: writes `postId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.postId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Frame the Assignment · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Unpack Sections · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Review Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below — downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to MySQL

### Persist Deliverable to MySQL

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Serialize Run Output (CSV) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (MySQL)

### Append Audit Row (MySQL)

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 MySQL connection

# Generate blog drafts with MySQL and WordPress in real time

**Pattern:** Content Generation · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Generate blog drafts with MySQL and WordPress in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual cross-posting is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Stamp & Shape Post → Payload Valid? → Frame the Assignment → Coordinator Agent → Unpack Sections → Persist Deliverable to MySQL → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to content generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating cross-posting in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; MySQL connection; OpenRouter API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The Coordinator Agent never does the specialist work itself — each specialist is delegated to its own sub-workflow via a Workflow Tool, so every specialist stays single-responsibility and testable in isolation instead of one giant prompt trying to do everything. An output parser forces the final answer into JSON so downstream nodes can rely on the shape instead of parsing free text.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a third specialist tool and update the system message to explain when the agent should reach for it.
2. Loosen or tighten the JSON schema on the output parser and see how the agent's final answer shape changes.
3. Replace one placeholder Workflow Tool with a real n8n sub-workflow and measure the round-trip latency.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your cross-posting process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
